Primary Endpoint(s): {"Primary Endpoint(s)": "Overall survival assessed in the intention-to-treat population (defined as the time from randomisation to death from any cause)."}
Secondary Endpoint(s): {"Secondary Endpoint(s)": "Prostate cancer-specific survival (defined as the time from randomisation to death from prostate cancer); failure-free survival (defined as biochemical failure, local or lymph node progression, distant metastases, or death from prostate cancer); progression-free survival (defined as failure-free survival but excluding biochemical failure); metastatic progression-free survival (defined as progression of or new distant metastases or death from prostate cancer); symptomatic skeletal-related events (defined as pathological fracture or spinal cord compression or bone pain requiring radiotherapy or surgery); toxic effects and adverse events; and cost-effectiveness of research treatment."}

Type of Therapy: {"Type of Therapy": "Participants in the clinical trial received standard of care, which included androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) via luteinising hormone-releasing hormone agonists/antagonists or bilateral orchiectomy. From December 17, 2015, docetaxel (75 mg/m² intravenously for six cycles) with prednisolone (10 mg orally once daily) was permitted as part of standard of care after starting ADT. Palliative radiotherapy was also allowed. Experimental arms included standard of care plus either abiraterone acetate (1000 mg orally once daily) and prednisolone (5 mg orally once daily), or standard of care plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg orally once daily), prednisolone (5 mg orally once daily), and enzalutamide (160 mg orally once daily)."}
